hypothesis:
HYPOTHESIS: The application of compression mechanisms inspired by peritoneal shunt treatment modalities in biology can improve the efficiency of context compression in transformer-based question answering models.

PREDICTION: We expect to find a 25% reduction in inference complexity and a 15% increase in question answering accuracy when using the proposed compression mechanism compared to the baseline model.

EXPERIMENT: To test this hypothesis, we will implement a transformer-based question answering model with a custom compression module inspired by peritoneal shunt treatment modalities and evaluate its performance on a benchmark dataset, such as SQuAD. The model will be trained and tested using a leave-one-out strategy to assess its efficiency and accuracy.

IMPACT: This research matters because it has the potential to significantly improve the scalability and accuracy of question answering models, enabling their deployment on edge devices and enhancing their ability to provide reliable answers to complex queries.
